data.title: EU to Apply 3-Euro Custom Duty on Small Parcels From July 2026
data.description:
By Edith Hancock

European Union member states agreed to temporarily add a 3-euro ($3.52) customs duty on small parcels entering the bloc next year as part of a push to stem the surge of cheap goods from countries like China.

The European Council--which represents the interests of each of the 27 EU countries--said on Friday that diplomats agreed to apply a fixed duty on small parcels valued at less than 150 euros from July 1, 2026.

The temporary rate will apply to packages sent directly to consumers from non-EU countries. A statement from the council on Friday said that would affect around 93% of e-commerce shipments into the EU.

"With e-commerce expanding rapidly, the world is changing fast - and we need the right tools to keep pace," Maros Sefcovic, the EU's trade commissioner, said on Friday.

It comes as officials are still working to reform the bloc's customs rules in a broader pushback against low-value products flooding the market. EU finance ministers agreed in November to remove a duty-free rule that allowed goods valued at less than 150 euros to enter Europe duty free. The change is expected to come into effect in 2028, but the EU institutions have faced pressure to move quickly.

"Given the rapid increase in e-commerce goods being imported into the EU, the commission and member states have together acknowledged the need for an urgent solution," the commission said in a statement Friday.

The EU is also discussing a separate handling fee as part of the commission's customs-reform package.

Write to Edith Hancock at edith.hancock@wsj.com

data.title: 10,530,000,000 Dogecoin Registered as Futures Traders Gear Up
data.description:
Dogecoin is showing signs of a major rebound amid rising interest on its derivatives market. The negative trend witnessed in DOGE futures' activity has seen a big shift as the asset sees a decent increase in its open interest volume over the past day.

According to data from CoinGlass, Dogecoin futures traders have committed over 10.5 billion DOGE worth over $1.48 billion on Friday, Dec. 12. This marks a decent increase of 1.08% in the meme asset’s open interest over the last day.

Dogecoin prints bullish signal

Despite being in red territory in the last few days, Dogecoin's open interest has shifted in the past few hours, suggesting a major rebound could be imminent. 

While Dogecoin open interest is a tool that measures how much investors are willing to bet on the asset, an increase in the metric reflects a bullish signal for the DOGE price.

According to data from CoinMarketCap, Dogecoin has surged by 2.16% in the last 24 hours. After seeing an intraday low of $0.1365, Dogecoin is now trading at $0.1409 as of press time.

This metric defies the trend witnessed across the broad crypto derivatives market as major cryptocurrencies, including XRP, have seen their open interest decline over the last day.

Following the rising interest from investors, Dogecoin appears to be headed for a big price rebound.

Nonetheless, the data shows that traders on Gate.io exchange have shown the most interest as the exchange shows the highest percent of traders betting on the asset.

Notably, Gate.io accounts for 24.82% of total open interest, while Binance comes second, accounting for 18.38% with a massive 1,930,000,000 DOGE tokens committed on the exchange.

data.title: Mergers and acquisitions
data.description:
The following bids, mergers, acquisitions and disposals were reported by 1430 GMT on Friday:

** SolGold <SOLG.L> said it was "minded to recommend" an improved offer from its top shareholder, Jiangxi Copper <600362.SS> that values the gold and copper miner at about 842 million pounds ($1.13 billion), amid a global race for copper assets.

** Czech billionaire investor Daniel Kretinsky's EP Equity Investment has agreed to buy central European used-car dealer Aures Holdings, the companies said .

** British money manager Aberdeen <ABDN.L> said it would acquire the management of nine U.S.-based closed-end funds with assets totalling 1.5 billion pounds ($2 billion), part of an ongoing drive to expand in the U.S. market.

** Retailers Destination XL Group <DXLG.O> and FullBeauty Brands agreed to merge, creating a bigger player in the size-inclusive apparel market, the companies announced on Thursday, confirming an earlier Reuters report.

** BNP Paribas <BNPP.PA> has entered exclusive talks to sell its 67% stake in its Moroccan unit BMCI to Holmarcom Group, France's biggest bank said.

data.title: Data center REIT Fermi tumbles 40% after potential customer backs out of funding deal
data.description:
Shares of Fermi plunged 40% on Friday after the data center real estate investment company said a prospective tenant had terminated a deal to help fund construction at its Texas site, dealing a setback to the newly listed firm.

Under the terms of the agreement, the undisclosed customer would have leased part of Fermi's Project Matador site in Texas and could provide up to $150 million to help finance construction.

While both sides remain in discussions over a potential lease, the funding agreement has been scrapped.

The move could mark an early challenge to Fermi, a company co-founded by former U.S. Energy Secretary Rick Perry, which started trading in October at a valuation of $14.8 billion but is yet to bring in revenue.

Fermi rode strong AI-driven demand to a robust IPO in October as investors piled into companies providing the infrastructure necessary for the technology.

But the latest setback for the company, which is less than a year old, comes at a time when it is navigating a more cautious backdrop for AI as some investors question how long the sector's rally can continue without clearer profit paths.

The stock was last trading at $9.08, 57% lower than its initial public offering price of $21.

RNS Number : 4083L Oxford Nanopore Technologies plc 12 December 2025  

12 December 2025

Oxford Nanopore Technologies plc (the "Company")

Share Incentive Plan ("SIP") PDMR Notification

Notification and public disclosure of transactions by persons discharging managerial responsibilities and persons closely associated with them

The Company has been notified by Computershare Trustees Limited, the trustee of the SIP (the "SIP Trustee") that on 12 December 2025, it made an award of Partnership Shares (as defined in the SIP) of 118 ordinary shares of £0.0001 ("Ordinary Shares") on behalf of Nick Keher, a director of the Company and a Person Discharging Managerial Responsibilities ("PDMR"). The Ordinary Shares were purchased using each participating employee's gross salary at a price of £1.27 per Ordinary Share.

Under the SIP, the SIP Trustee will award each participating employee one Matching Share (as defined in the SIP) for each Ordinary Share purchased by the employee under the SIP. On 12 December 2025, the Company issued 118 Ordinary Shares to the SIP Trustee to hold on behalf of Nick Keher to satisfy the Matching Shares awarded under the SIP to him on that date.

data.title: Guiding Stars: Easy Snack Options for Busy Holidays
data.description:
SCARBOROUGH, ME / ACCESS Newswire / December 12, 2025 / Image by Freepik

Originally published on Guiding Stars Health & Nutrition News

by Allison Stowell

The holiday season can be an endurance event. Days often include extra errands or commitments, and somehow it seems like the usual errands and commitments just take longer, adding to an exhausting day. When life gets busy (likely interrupting your meals), I highly recommend planning balanced snacks. If not, you may find yourself grabbing fast food or a decadent choice as an afternoon pick-me-up.

Star-Worthy Snacks

Here are some ideas to stay energized while shopping, wrapping, decorating, traveling, or trying to make it to your child's poorly timed school event. Whether you're at home or on the go, there's a snack for that:

  • Single serve guacamole with Guiding Stars earning multigrain chips

  • Sliced cheese, low-sodium turkey breast, and Guiding Stars earning crackers

  • Guiding Stars earning toasted whole grain waffle topped with chocolate sunflower seed butter

  • High-protein yogurt drink

  • Guiding Stars earning oatmeal cup topped with nut butter and seasonal fruit

  • Sliced banana and peanut butter (for a more filling snack, spread on a slice of whole grain toast)

  • Single hummus serving with carrots, sugar snap peas, grape tomatoes

  • No-sugar-added, Guiding Stars earning Greek yogurt topped with 1/3 cup high-protein granola

  • "Sippable" soup like tomato or butternut squash soup that can be enjoyed in a to-go cup

  • Mashed avocado, sliced hard-boiled egg, and sliced baguette

  • Small serving of blueberries and lightly salted almonds

  • Granola bar (aim for 7 grams protein, 5 or more grams fat, minimal saturated fat, less added sugar)

Keep an Eye on Your Beverages

This time of year, it's easy to have beverages with more added sugar and calories than we realize. Consider your choice before ordering a coffee drink, picking up a bottled beverage, or going for a mocktail. Choose an alternative or adjust ingredients if needed to reduce the calories, added sugar, or saturated fat in your drink.

About Guiding Stars

Guiding Stars is an objective, evidence-based, nutrition guidance program that evaluates foods and beverages to make nutritious choices simple. Products that meet transparent nutrition criteria earn a 1, 2, or 3 star rating for good, better, and best nutrition. Guiding Stars can be found in more than 2,000 grocery stores and through the Guiding Stars Food Finder app.

data.title: Five Ways to Style a Suit So You Don't Look Like a Corporate Drone — WSJ
data.description:
By WSJ Off Duty Fashion Team | Photography by Elizabeth Arvelos Coetzee

Styling a suit with a crisp white shirt is a fail-safe move, but it often reads "corporate square." To come off cooler and breezier in 2026, pair suits with henleys, unexpected knitwear or button-downs in shades that match the tailoring. Done right, you'll look neither stuffy nor sloppy — the relaxed-suit sweet spot. Here, five fresh moves.

1. Add a Layer — and Undo Buttons

Our first look comes off a little youthful and insouciant. And great news: Most guys will already have the ingredients in their closet. Start with a ribbed tank or a thin white cotton tee underneath a button-down shirt, liberally undo the shirt buttons (leave just the bottom two or three fastened), and tuck both shirts into the pants. A subtle Western belt finishes off the look. This just-outta-bed styling works equally well with a casual single-breasted suit or, as shown here, a floppy double-breasted number worn open, the modern way.

2. Swap in a Henley

The henley, that old-school, manly staple, has roared back into fashion lately. Though typically worn in the most casual settings, henleys work surprisingly well under tailoring. Consider this a more rugged, less-clichéd alternative to a white tee under a suit. Choose a shirt that's not overly flimsy; you don't want the unbuttoned placket flopping like an overcooked noodle under the blazer. Here, we've gone for a ribbed version in white, though a color that matches the suit would also do the job.

3. Reach for a Chill Three-Piece

Sure, three-piece suits call to mind jittery grooms and stilted royals. But matching vests and suits don't have to be a whole "thing." The best casual takes deliver a hit of the unexpected without appearing overly fussy. Here, a navy wool combo from California Arts, an up-and-coming brand, skews minimalist thanks to the vest's hidden buttons. (You could wear the vest with other navy suits, provided the fabrics are similar.) To relax the look, slip a tee underneath and a pendant necklace on top.

4. Match the Shades

Tone-on-tone dressing is a foolproof way to elevate any menswear outfit, including tailoring. Because no one needs an eyeful of orange or royal blue, stick to shirts and suits in muted shades — gray, navy, brown. The shirt needn't match the suit exactly, just keep it in the same color family. Try a textured button-down — here, we've chosen a wool flannel example — so that the monochrome outfit doesn't fall flat.

5. Play With Knitwear

Fine knitwear is the ideal smart-casual suit accompaniment. Look for a luxe quarter-zip sweater, a thin turtleneck or a merino polo — all read easy yet pulled-together. Overachievers wanting oomph could layer the turtleneck under the polo, as shown here. Keep the knitwear items in the same neutral shade for a cohesive feel — and stick to fine-merino designs to avoid overheating. We've chosen navy to make the pale-pink tailoring pop.

data.title: GOODBYE KANSAS SECURES MAJOR ORDER FOR GLOBALLY RENOWNED GAME IP
data.description:
Goodbye Kansas has been awarded a substantial new order, including a high-end CGI trailer and Key Art production, from a leading international games developer for a highly successful and well-known entertainment franchise. The total order value amounts to close to EUR 1.6 million, representing a significant new contract.

The contract consists of two separate, major orders, concerning the same globally renowned game IP. The first order focuses on the creation of high-quality character and object renders, which will be used primarily as promotional Key Arts and related CGI assets. The second, more comprehensive order, is for the production of a high-quality CGI trailer, to the standard Goodbye Kansas is celebrated for, along with the delivery of various high-end CGI assets.

“Being awarded this project demonstrates the industry’s strong trust in our team’s solid capabilities in Key Art creation and cinematic CGI trailer development. This multi-project deal for a global franchise of this caliber solidifies our position as a leading creative partner to the world’s biggest publishers," says Thomas Lindgren, CEO of Goodbye Kansas.

The order value of almost EUR 1.6 million will be recognized as revenue over the production period, where the project will start immediately and is expected to finish in the second quarter, 2026.

For further information, please contact: Thomas Lindgren, Acting CEO, Goodbye Kansas Group

E-mail: thomas.lindgren@goodbyekansas.com

data.title: UBS Stock Hits New Post-Crisis High — WSJ
data.description:
By WSJ Staff

Stock in UBS is back to highs last seen before the global financial crisis hammered it and other banks.

The Swiss bank's shares jumped as much as 5% on Friday, reaching the highest intraday level since February 2008, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

The runup partly reflects a broader rally in financial stocks, and also investors warming to UBS's prospects after its rescue takeover of crosstown rival Credit Suisse in 2023.

The latest catalyst was news that Swiss legislators are proposing bank capital requirements that could be less drastic than previously feared.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).
